goal: Add the missing program-only enforcement surface to AgentActionGuard for guarded SOL transfers, including attestation state, authorized attestor writes, guarded transfer execution, Anchor tests, and any generated IDL or client typing needed by the program test workflow.

context.summary: The existing AgentActionGuard program already defines UserPolicy, ActionApproval, and conditional oracle execution in back/solana/agent-action-guard/programs/agent-action-guard/src/lib.rs, but it does not implement guarded_transfer, WalletSafetyAttestation, or Anchor tests for guarded transfer enforcement. The broader on-chain integration spec remains in docs/wallet-safety-validation-onchain-enforcement/ and should consume this program-only spec rather than duplicate it.

acceptanceCriteria: AgentActionGuard defines a guarded_transfer instruction that requires the user signer, validates UserPolicy.enabled, validates ActionApproval for the exact transfer, validates WalletSafetyAttestation for the same user, recipient, and action_hash, performs a CPI to SystemProgram::transfer, and marks ActionApproval.executed = true in the same instruction.
AgentActionGuard defines WalletSafetyAttestation and a gated upsert_wallet_safety_attestation instruction that only authorized attestors can call.
The program defines one canonical action_hash format for guarded SOL transfers and documents the mismatch risk with the current off-chain hash format.

Anchor tests cover the happy path and the required failure paths: wrong PDA seeds, mismatched recipient, mismatched amount, mismatched action_hash, expired approval, revoked approval, already executed approval, missing attestation, expired attestation, unauthorized attestor, and replay.
Generated IDL and any required typed client artifacts include the new program surface needed by the Anchor tests.
constraints: Docs only in this handoff; do not implement runtime or program code.
Scope is strictly program-only plus Anchor tests and generated IDL/client typing required by those tests.
Do not make this spec own frontend or backend integration migration beyond noting the dependency on current direct SystemProgram.transfer usage.
Keep work focused on back/solana/agent-action-guard/** and generated artifacts required by that program workflow.
All tasks must remain in status pending with empty evidence arrays.
decisions: Use a minimal GuardConfig PDA with admin, paused, and authorized attestors for MVP instead of hardcoding attestors or requiring co-signing on every user transfer.
Use a canonical WalletSafetyAttestation PDA derived from user, recipient, and action_hash so attestation reuse across different transfers is rejected by construction.
Treat this spec as a program dependency of docs/wallet-safety-validation-onchain-enforcement/, not as a replacement for the broader integration spec.
Define one canonical guarded SOL transfer action_hash format with a domain separator plus ordered byte encoding of user, recipient, amount_lamports, approval_expires_at, and cluster.

tasks.title: Define canonical guarded transfer hashing and PDA bindings
tasks.description: Document and implement a single guarded SOL transfer action_hash format plus the PDA seed scheme for GuardConfig, ActionApproval, and WalletSafetyAttestation so all program tests and generated clients use the same bindings.
verification: The guarded SOL transfer hash format is defined once and reused consistently in program tests.
PDA seeds for ActionApproval and WalletSafetyAttestation are deterministic and verified in tests.

tasks.title: Add GuardConfig account and attestor governance instructions
tasks.description: Introduce the minimal config PDA and the instruction surface required to initialize and update the set of authorized attestors plus the paused flag.
verification: The program exposes a config account that stores admin, paused, and authorized attestors.
Only the admin can update attestor authorization state.

tasks.title: Add WalletSafetyAttestation account and upsert instruction
tasks.description: Define WalletSafetyAttestation storage and implement upsert_wallet_safety_attestation gated by the authorized attestor model.
verification: Attestations are bound to user, recipient, and action_hash with an enforced TTL.

tasks.title: Implement guarded_transfer enforcement path
tasks.description: Add guarded_transfer to validate the user policy, approval, attestation, and exact transfer parameters before performing the SystemProgram transfer CPI and marking the approval executed.
verification: The CPI transfer and executed flag update happen in the same successful instruction path.
Replay and approval or attestation mismatches are rejected.

tasks.title: Add Anchor tests for guarded transfer success and failure matrix
tasks.description: Create program tests that cover the happy path plus wrong PDA seeds, mismatched recipient, mismatched amount, mismatched action_hash, expired approval, revoked approval, already executed approval, missing attestation, expired attestation, unauthorized attestor, and replay.
verification: Anchor tests exist for the happy path and every required rejection path.
Tests prove replay prevention after one successful guarded transfer.

tasks.title: Regenerate IDL and typed client artifacts needed by tests
tasks.description: Regenerate the Anchor IDL and any typed TS client output required by the repository's program test workflow so the new instructions and accounts are consumable from tests.
verification: The IDL includes guarded_transfer and upsert_wallet_safety_attestation.
Any generated typed client used by tests reflects the new account and instruction surface.

risks: The current off-chain action_hash format may not match the new canonical guarded transfer hash, which can block integration until the broader runtime spec adopts the same format.
A poorly bounded authorized_attestors list could create account sizing or upgrade friction inside GuardConfig.
If warn semantics are not confirmed, implementation and tests may disagree on whether warn permits execution.
validation: Review the new docs against the explorer handoff to confirm the scope stays program-only.
Confirm docs/wallet-safety-validation-onchain-enforcement/technical-spec.md references this program-only spec as a dependency.
